summary: A monumental stepped pyramid structure with three tall dark towers at its corners and base. The building features alternating horizontal bands of dark stone and light wood/stone, creating a striped appearance. A central stepped pyramid rises from the center, flanked by three prominent dark towers with flat tops. The entire structure sits on a white block foundation perimeter.

shape.overall_form: Stepped pyramid with three corner towers
shape.symmetry: Asymmetrical; three towers positioned at different corners

rebuild_hints: Build three separate dark stone towers of varying heights (tallest ~18 blocks)
Create central stepped pyramid with alternating dark and light horizontal layers
Apply horizontal striping pattern across entire structure using wood and stone alternately
Place white block foundation perimeter around entire footprint
Add flat caps with overhang to each tower top
Include small window openings distributed vertically on tower faces
Ensure towers are positioned at asymmetrical corners rather than symmetrically
Step the pyramid inward as it rises, creating distinct horizontal ledges
uncertainties: Exact tower heights relative to each other - appear to vary but precise block count unclear
Specific wood type (oak, spruce, etc.) cannot be determined from image
Internal structure and hollow vs solid composition unknown
Exact number of horizontal striping bands - approximately 8-10 visible but some may be partial
Whether windows are actual openings or decorative blocks
Precise footprint dimensions - estimated from visual proportions
